report_name: WatchGuard: Shadow AI, Password Reuse Fueling Business Cyber Risk

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Password reuse and sharing by employees provide attackers with legitimate credentials to access corporate systems.

attack_patterns.name: Credential Stuffing
attack_patterns.description: Widespread password reuse makes organizations highly vulnerable to credential stuffing attacks, where credentials from one breach are used to compromise other accounts.

attack_patterns.name: Drive-by Compromise
attack_patterns.description: Employees using insecure public Wi-Fi for work can be exposed to compromised networks that redirect them to malicious sites, leading to a drive-by compromise.

attack_patterns.name: Transfer Data to Cloud Account
attack_patterns.description: The use of unapproved 'Shadow AI' tools by employees involves transferring potentially sensitive corporate data to external cloud-based AI services.

attack_patterns.name: Adversary-in-the-Middle
attack_patterns.description: Employees using insecure public Wi-Fi for work without a VPN expose corporate data to interception via Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks.

identities.name: Small and mid-sized businesses (SMBs)
identities.description: A class of businesses identified as being at high risk due to employee behaviors like shadow AI usage and poor password hygiene, as detailed in the WatchGuard 2026 Cybersecurity Hygiene Report.

infrastructure.name: Public AI Services
infrastructure.description: Unauthorized public AI services (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) used by employees for work tasks, creating a risk of sensitive corporate data leakage. This phenomenon is termed 'Shadow AI'.

infrastructure.name: Insecure Public Wi-Fi
infrastructure.description: Insecure public Wi-Fi networks used by employees for work, exposing corporate data to interception via Man-in-the-Middle attacks.

indicators.name: Shadow AI Service DNS Queries
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Shadow AI Service DNS Queries
id: 2b1c8e9f-4d5a-4b6c-8f9a-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to popular public AI services. This may indicate 'Shadow AI' usage, where employees use unsanctioned AI tools for work, potentially leading to data leakage.
references:
  - https://www.watchguard.com/wgrd-news/press-releases/employees-drive-rising-cybersecurity-risk-shadow-ai-and-unsafe-work-habits
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
tags:
  - attack.t1537
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'openai.com'
      - 'anthropic.com'
      - 'gemini.google.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: This indicator detects DNS queries to common public AI services, which can be a sign of 'Shadow AI' usage within an organization, as described in the WatchGuard report.

indicators.name: Potential Credential Stuffing Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Credential Stuffing Activity
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4a3b-2c1d-0e9f8a7b6c5d
status: experimental
description: 'Detects a high rate of failed authentication attempts from a single source IP across multiple user accounts, which is indicative of a credential stuffing attack. This rule is conceptual and requires a SIEM with correlation capabilities.'
references:
  - https://www.watchguard.com/wgrd-news/press-releases/employees-drive-rising-cybersecurity-risk-shadow-ai-and-unsafe-work-habits
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-14
tags:
  - attack.t1110.004
level: high
logsource:
  category: authentication
  product: generic
detection:
  selection:
    event_type: 'failure'
  condition: selection | count(username) by source_ip > 10
indicators.description: This behavioral indicator describes the logic for detecting credential stuffing attacks, as highlighted as a major risk in the report. It looks for a high rate of failed logins from a single IP across many accounts.
